name: Zeelandic
notes: Zeelandic (Zeêuws), a West Germanic (Low Franconian) variety of Zeeland and Goeree-Overflakkee in the Netherlands. STUB: the grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified. Zeelandic is written in the Latin script using ad-hoc Dutch-based spelling conventions; it has no official orthography and no published letter-to-phoneme table, and its dialects differ in the realisation of the falling diphthongs. Documented features (retained monophthongs [i] and [y] for ⟨ij⟩ and ⟨ui⟩, falling diphthongs, h-dropping) are not enough to build an honest grapheme map, so none is claimed. A Dutch-orthography-based input is assumed for any future spec.

ancestors.notes: Germanic — the classification node this language sits under.
